de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.label

Class ParameterlessTermLabel

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.label.ParameterlessTermLabel
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    TermLabel, Named

    public final class ParameterlessTermLabel
extends Object
implements TermLabel
    The Class ParameterlessTermLabel can be used to define labels without parameters. You can use a SingletonLabelFactory to create a factory for an instance of this class.
    Author:
    mattias ulbrich
    See Also:
    SingletonLabelFactory

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      static TermLabel ANON_HEAP_LABEL
      Label attached to anonymisation heap function symbols as for instance introduce in UseOperationContractRule or WhileInvariantRule.
      static Name ANON_HEAP_LABEL_NAME
      Name of ANON_HEAP_LABEL.
      static TermLabel IMPLICIT_SPECIFICATION_LABEL
      Label attached to a term which is specified implicitly (by the JML specification).
      static Name IMPLICIT_SPECIFICATION_LABEL_NAME
      Name of IMPLICIT_SPECIFICATION_LABEL.
      private Name name
      The unique name of this label.
      static TermLabel POST_CONDITION_LABEL

      Label attached to the post condition.

      Label attached to skolem constants introduced by the rule pullOutSelect.

      Label attached to the post condition.

      Name of SELF_COMPOSITION_LABEL.
      static TermLabel SHORTCUT_EVALUATION_LABEL
      Label attached to a term with the logical operator '||' or '&&' to distinguish from '|' or '&' respectively.
      static Name SHORTCUT_EVALUATION_LABEL_NAME
      Name of SHORTCUT_EVALUATION_LABEL.
      static TermLabel UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL
      Label attached to a term which denotes an undefined value.
      static Name UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL_NAME
      Name of UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL.

      • SHORTCUT_EVALUATION_LABEL

        public static final TermLabel SHORTCUT_EVALUATION_LABEL
        Label attached to a term with the logical operator '||' or '&&' to distinguish from '|' or '&' respectively.

      • UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL_NAME

        public static final Name UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL_NAME
        Name of UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL.

      • UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL

        public static final TermLabel UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL
        Label attached to a term which denotes an undefined value. At present it is only used for the else-part of the #IfExThenElse operator, when it is used for the translation of JML's \min and \max operator. It is necessary to evaluate this constant expression to be not well-defined.

      • SELF_COMPOSITION_LABEL_NAME

        public static final Name SELF_COMPOSITION_LABEL_NAME
        Name of SELF_COMPOSITION_LABEL.

      • SELF_COMPOSITION_LABEL

        public static final TermLabel SELF_COMPOSITION_LABEL
        Label attached to the post condition.

      • POST_CONDITION_LABEL_NAME

        public static final Name POST_CONDITION_LABEL_NAME
        Label attached to the post condition.

      • POST_CONDITION_LABEL

        public static final TermLabel POST_CONDITION_LABEL

      • name

        private final Name name
        The unique name of this label. This is the basename and does not include the parameters

    • Constructor Detail

      • ParameterlessTermLabel

        public ParameterlessTermLabel(Name name)
        Instantiates a new simple term label.
        Parameters:
        name - the name, not null
        instantiator - the fixed associated instantiator, may be null.

    • Method Detail

      • name

        public Name name()
        Description copied from interface: Named
        returns the name of this element
        Specified by:
        name in interface Named
        Returns:
        the name of the element

      • getChild

        public Object getChild(int i)
        Retrieves the i-th parameter object of this term label.

        A term label may have structure, i.e. can be parameterized.

        Simple term labels have no parameters. This always throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException.

        Specified by:
        getChild in interface TermLabel
        Parameters:
        i - the number of the parameter to retrieve ( 0 <= i < getChildCount())
        Returns:
        the selected parameter

      • getChildCount

        public int getChildCount()
        Gets the number of parameters of this term label.

        Simple term labels have no parameters. This always returns 0.

        Specified by:
        getChildCount in interface TermLabel
        Returns:
        the number of parameters (a non-negative number)

      • toString

        public String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class Object

      • equals

        public boolean equals(Object obj)

        This object is equal to another ParameterlessTermLabel iff they bear the same name.

        Overrides:
        equals in class Object
